expected_answer.root_cause: When a container entrypoint is a shell script without a proper shebang line, the Linux kernel's execve() in runc's process_linux.go falls back to /bin/sh. On multi-arch images, /bin/sh may be a binary of the wrong architecture (e.g., amd64 on an arm64 node), producing 'exec format error'. The error propagates from runc through containerd shim to Docker daemon without additional architecture context.
error_chain: Docker daemon start.go initiates container start via containerd gRPC API
containerd creates a shim process via pkg/process/init.go
containerd shim delegates process execution to runc
runc process_linux.go calls execve() with the container entrypoint
Linux kernel attempts to execute the script, falls back to /bin/sh due to missing shebang
/bin/sh is a wrong-architecture binary, kernel returns ENOEXEC (exec format error)
runc wraps the error and returns it to containerd shim
containerd propagates the error to Docker daemon
Docker daemon returns 'OCI runtime create failed' to the user
affected_components: All multi-arch images with shell script entrypoints missing shebang lines
expected_answer.remediation: Ensure all entrypoint scripts include a proper shebang line (e.g., #!/bin/sh). For platform operators, add image validation that checks entrypoint executability and shebang presence. Consider adding architecture-aware error messages in runc to distinguish between 'wrong architecture' and 'missing shebang' failures.
